schools.summary: Gill: Joshua was probably about a hundred, living to a hundred and ten; the land was seven years in dividing; very much land yet remained.

themes: Joshua was old... and there remaineth yet very much land to be possessed
the promise outrunning one life's labour
not as though I had already attained... but I follow after (Phil. 3:12)

schools.summary: Gill: the account given for Joshua's information, to know what to divide, and for Israel's, to know their right; it begins with the Philistine borders.

themes: This is the land that yet remaineth: all the borders of the Philistines, and all Geshuri
the whole promised land reckoned Israel's by right
he is faithful that promised (Heb. 10:23)

schools.summary: Gill: Sihor, which Jarchi and Kimchi take for the Nile, named for its black waters; it reached to Ekron, the five Philistine lords.

themes: From Sihor, which is before Egypt, even unto the borders of Ekron... the five lords of the Philistines
the inheritance contested by a stubborn enemy
through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of God (Acts 14:22)

schools.summary: Gill: the Canaanites particularly so called, distinct from the other nations, dwelling in several parts; the land reached to the Amorite borders.

themes: From the south, all the land of the Canaanites... unto the borders of the Amorites
how much of the promise was still unrealized
reaching forth unto those things which are before (Phil. 3:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the land of the Giblites, called Byblus by the Greeks, pleasantly situated by the seaside; it reached from Baal-gad to the entering of Hamath.

themes: the land of the Giblites, and all Lebanon... unto the entering into Hamath
the largeness of God's promised inheritance
able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask (Eph. 3:20)

schools.summary: Gill: the inhabitants of the hill country in and about Lebanon.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD promising to drive out the Sidonians and all, only divide it by lot.

themes: them will I drive out from before the children of Israel: only divide thou it by lot
faith in the promise joined with present obedience
the LORD thy God... will not fail thee (Deut. 31:6)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua, having God's command and authority, was to set about it at once with all diligence.
Henry: the orders given Joshua to assign each tribe its inheritance.

themes: divide this land for an inheritance unto the nine tribes, and the half tribe of Manasseh
God appointing to each his own inheritance
the lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places (Ps. 16:6)

schools.summary: Gill: this was with the other half of Manasseh, those settled beyond Jordan; Moses the servant of the LORD had given them their inheritance eastward.

themes: the Reubenites and the Gadites have received their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond Jordan eastward
God's ordered provision, each in his appointed place
God is not unrighteous to forget your work (Heb. 6:10)

schools.summary: Gill: Aroer, a city of Moab, the river Arnon the border between Moab and the Amorites.
Keil & Delitzsch: the description from Aroer and the plain of Medeba.

themes: From Aroer, that is upon the bank of the river Arnon... and all the plain of Medeba unto Dibon
the exactness with which God assigns each portion
the LORD... thou maintainest my lot (Ps. 16:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Heshbon, a city Sihon took from the Moabites and made his royal seat; the territory reached to the border of Ammon.

themes: all the cities of Sihon king of the Amorites, which reigned in Heshbon
the realm of a conquered king turned to Israel's inheritance
the meek shall inherit the earth (Ps. 37:11)

schools.summary: Gill: Gilead was part of the kingdom of Og, half given to Reuben and half to Gad.
Clarke and Wesley: the borders of the Geshurites and Maachathites.

themes: And Gilead, and the border of the Geshurites and Maachathites, and all mount Hermon, and all Bashan unto Salcah
the bounty of God's provision, a rich heritage
God... giveth us richly all things to enjoy (1 Tim. 6:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Og reigning in Ashtaroth and Edrei.
Keil & Delitzsch: Og remaining of the remnant of the giants, whom Moses smote and cast out.

themes: All the kingdom of Og in Bashan... who remained of the remnant of the giants: for these did Moses smite
no enemy too mighty for the LORD to subdue
the LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters (Ps. 93:4)

schools.summary: Gill: they expelled them neither in the times of Moses nor of Joshua; they dwelt among the Israelites until this day.

themes: the children of Israel expelled not the Geshurites, nor the Maachathites
the danger of partial obedience leaving a snare
they were mingled among the heathen, and learned their works (Ps. 106:34-35)

schools.summary: Gill: neither the Lord nor Moses appointed Levi any land inheritance, for the Lord was to be their inheritance. Calvin: Levi's portion in the LORD.

themes: unto the tribe of Levi he gave none inheritance; the sacrifices of the LORD... are their inheritance
those who serve God find their portion in him
the LORD is the portion of mine inheritance (Ps. 16:5)

schools.summary: Gill: given according to the number of them, and sufficient for them.
Keil & Delitzsch: Moses giving Reuben its inheritance.

themes: Moses gave unto the tribe of the children of Reuben inheritance according to their families
the land measured to fit each tribe
distribution was made unto every man according as he had need (Acts 4:35)

schools.summary: Gill: the country of Sihon so described, showing it was his land given to Reuben, though not all of it.
Keil & Delitzsch: the coast from Aroer and the plain by Medeba.

themes: their coast was from Aroer, that is on the bank of the river Arnon... and all the plain by Medeba
God's exact and sure dividing of each portion
he hath determined... the bounds of their habitation (Acts 17:26)

schools.summary: Gill: Heshbon and her cities in the plain, by Medeba, reaching to Dibon.
Clarke: Bamoth-baal, the high places of Baal, from altars on hills for idolatrous worship.

themes: Heshbon, and all her cities... Dibon, and Bamothbaal, and Bethbaalmeon
God giving his people to inherit and cleanse what sin had polluted
come out from among them, and be ye separate (2 Cor. 6:15-17)

schools.summary: Gill: Jahazah, called Jahaz, where the battle was fought between Sihon and Israel.
Clarke: Jahaza was given to the Levites (1 Chr. 6:78).

themes: And Jahazah, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath
the field of battle turned to peaceable dwelling
they shall sit every man under his vine (Mic. 4:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Kirjathaim, of which he speaks elsewhere.
Clarke: it passed from the Emim to the Moabites, to the Amorites, and so to Israel.

themes: And Kirjathaim, and Sibmah, and Zarethshahar in the mount of the valley
the much-contested land settling where God appointed
he removeth kings, and setteth up kings (Dan. 2:21)

schools.summary: Gill: Beth-peor, so called from Peor, the idol of the Moabites, likely having a temple; over against it the valley where Israel abode.
Keil & Delitzsch: Beth-peor among the cities.

themes: And Bethpeor, and Ashdothpisgah, and Bethjeshimoth
God redeeming the very scene of his people's sin
where sin abounded, grace did much more abound (Rom. 5:20)

schools.summary: Gill: the cities of the plain in the champaign country.
Clarke: refers to the war with Midian (Num. 31), whence this verse seems drawn.

themes: all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites... whom Moses smote with the princes of Midian
the LORD giving his people the lands of conquered foes
so let all thine enemies perish, O LORD (Judg. 5:31)

schools.summary: Gill: Balaam slain at the same time as the princes of Midian; Kimchi supposes he had returned to Midian for his wages of counsel against Israel.

themes: Balaam also the son of Beor, the soothsayer, did the children of Israel slay with the sword
the wretched end of the covetous false prophet
the way of Balaam, who loved the wages of unrighteousness (2 Pet. 2:15; Jude 11)

schools.summary: Gill: their eastern border was Aroer on the Arnon, their western the river Jordan.
Clarke: the cities and villages, the villages being unwalled hamlets.

themes: the border of the children of Reuben was Jordan... This was the inheritance of the children of Reuben
the LORD giving a settled and entire inheritance
the whole disposing of the lot is of the LORD (Prov. 16:33)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses gave it on the other side Jordan, as he did to Reuben.
Keil & Delitzsch: the inheritance of Gad according to their families.

themes: And Moses gave inheritance unto the tribe of Gad... according to their families
God's impartial provision for each tribe in turn
the members should have the same care one for another (1 Cor. 12:25)

schools.summary: Gill: Jazer, their southern coast, spoken of elsewhere as a city of Moab.
Keil & Delitzsch: the coast of Gad.

themes: their coast was Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the children of Ammon
God providing a portion fitting each according to need
my God shall supply all your need (Phil. 4:19)

schools.summary: Gill: this describes their border from south to north; Ramath-mizpeh, the same with Ramoth-gilead; it reached from Mahanaim to the border of Debir.

themes: from Heshbon unto Ramathmizpeh, and Betonim; and from Mahanaim unto the border of Debir
the inheritance ground hallowed by God's past dealings
Jacob said... This is God's host (Gen. 32:2)

schools.summary: Gill: Beth-aram, the same with Beth-haran.
Clarke: Beth-aram was rebuilt by Herod and called Livias, after Livia.

themes: in the valley, Betharam... even unto the edge of the sea of Chinnereth on the other side Jordan eastward
a definite heritage fixed by God's hand through changing ages
one generation passeth... but the earth abideth for ever (Eccl. 1:4)

schools.summary: Gill: this is the inheritance as described.
Keil & Delitzsch: the cities and villages of Gad.

themes: This is the inheritance of the children of Gad after their families, the cities, and their villages
the LORD's provision full and definite for each
all things are yours... and ye are Christ's (1 Cor. 3:21-23)

schools.summary: Gill: whether at their request or of himself, there being land enough, is not certain.
Clarke: the half tribe of Manasseh settled when the tribes were divided.

themes: And Moses gave inheritance unto the half tribe of Manasseh... by their families
the variety of God's appointments among his people
there are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit (1 Cor. 12:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Mahanaim, a place in the tribe of Gad, was the boundary that way.
Keil & Delitzsch: all Bashan and the kingdom of Og given to half Manasseh.

themes: their coast was from Mahanaim, all Bashan, all the kingdom of Og... threescore cities
the largeness of God's gift to his people
an inheritance among them which are sanctified (Acts 26:18)

schools.summary: Gill: the half of Gilead not given to Gad, which Og had possessed, given to Manasseh.
Wesley: the children of Machir, before called the children of Manasseh.

themes: half Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og... pertaining unto the children of Machir
God rewarding the labour and valour of his people
thou shalt eat the labour of thine hands: happy shalt thou be (Ps. 128:2)

schools.summary: Gill: particularly described, that each might know their proper portion.
Clarke: Moses had settled everything relating to these eastern tribes.

themes: These are the countries which Moses did distribute for inheritance in the plains of Moab
God making plain to his people what he has given them
I will give them an everlasting name (Isa. 56:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses gave Levi no inheritance on either side Jordan, but declared they should have none, though they were his own tribe — showing him disinterested; the LORD God of Israel was their inheritance.

themes: unto the tribe of Levi Moses gave not any inheritance: the LORD God of Israel was their inheritance
the highest portion of all, God himself the heritage
the LORD is my portion, saith my soul (Lam. 3:24)
